headline: Visa authorized a $125 million deposit into its U.S. litigation escrow account, triggering a downward adjustment to Class B-1 and B-2 conversion rates.
investor_impact: The $125 million funding of the litigation escrow acts economically like a share repurchase for Class A shareholders, reducing the as-converted share count by roughly 400,000 shares. While the EPS accretion is negligible given Visa's massive scale, it resolves a portion of retrospective U.S. litigation responsibilities at the expense of Class B shareholders.
key_figures: Class B-1 conversion rate decreased from 1.5491 to 1.5475
Class B-2 conversion rate decreased from 1.5108 to 1.5075
Class B-1 as-converted shares reduced by 7,880 to 7,482,834
Class B-2 as-converted shares reduced by 392,202 to 181,412,788
